info.name: Phase 1 - E2E Integration Tests
info.description:
End-to-end integration tests for StableBridge Platform Phase 1.

Covers the full merchant lifecycle across S11 (Onboarding), S13 (Merchant IAM), and S10 (API Gateway).

Prerequisites:
- All services running via docker-compose.dev.yml
- S11 on port 8081, S13 on port 8083, S10 on port 8080
- Mailpit on port 8025
- Kafka, PostgreSQL, Redis, Temporal all healthy

Run folders in order (01 through 07). Each folder builds on state created by previous folders.

item.name: 01 - Merchant Onboarding (S11)
item.description: Onboard a new merchant through S11: apply, KYB, webhook, activate.

exec: // Wait 3 seconds for Temporal workflow to process KYB
console.log('Waiting 3 seconds for Temporal workflow to start KYB...');

request.description: Health check used as a delay step. Pre-request script waits 5 seconds for Kafka event propagation to S13 and S10.

item.name: 02 - Verify Kafka Propagation

item.name: 03 - Email Scraping & Invitation (Mailpit)
item.description: Scrape the invitation email from Mailpit, extract the invitation token, and accept the invitation to create the first admin user.

item.name: 05 - Gateway Auth Chain (S10)
item.description:
Test the S10 API Gateway authentication chain: OAuth token, S13 JWT pass-through, API key creation and usage.

NOTE: oauthClientId and oauthClientSecret must be set before running this folder. They are auto-provisioned when S10 processes the merchant.activated event. Check S10 logs or the merchant response in folder 02 for the client credentials.

exec: pm.test('Status code is 200 OK (proves S10 validates S13 JWT via JWKS)', function () {
pm.test('Merchant data returned via S13 JWT', function () {
console.log('S10 accepted S13 user JWT. JWKS integration verified.');

request.description: Uses the S13-issued user JWT to access S10. This proves S10 can validate S13 JWTs via the JWKS endpoint.

item.name: 07 - Negative Cases
item.description: Test error handling, validation, and security edge cases across all three services.

exec: // Use the same registration number as the original merchant
// The original used REG-E2E-{{$timestamp}} but we store the
// exact value. We use a known duplicate approach.
// Since we can't retrieve the original regNumber easily,
// we submit with the same legalName + country combination
// which should trigger the duplicate check.
pm.test('Error response has meaningful message', function () {

body.raw:
{
  "legalName": "Duplicate Merchant Ltd",
  "tradingName": "DupeMerch",
  "registrationNumber": "REG-DUPLICATE-FIXED-001",
  "registrationCountry": "GB",
  "entityType": "PRIVATE_LIMITED",
  "websiteUrl": "https://duplicate-merchant.com",
  "primaryCurrency": "USD",
  "primaryContactEmail": "dup@testmerchant.com",
  "primaryContactName": "Dup User",
  "registeredAddress": {
    "streetLine1": "456 Dup Street",
    "city": "London",
    "postcode": "EC1A 1BB",
    "country": "GB"
  },
  "beneficialOwners": [
    {
      "fullName": "Dup User",
      "dateOfBirth": "1990-01-01",
      "nationality": "GB",
      "ownershipPct": 100.00,
      "isPoliticallyExposed": false
    }
  ],
  "requestedCorridors": ["GB->US"]
}

request.description:
Submit a merchant with a known duplicate registration number. First run this request once to create the record, then run it again with the same Idempotency-Key removed (a new one is generated) to trigger 409.

Alternative: Run the request twice in succession -- the second call with a new Idempotency-Key should return 409.

exec: pm.test('Status code is 409 Conflict (duplicate registration)', function () {

body.raw:
{
  "legalName": "Duplicate Merchant Ltd",
  "tradingName": "DupeMerch",
  "registrationNumber": "REG-DUPLICATE-FIXED-001",
  "registrationCountry": "GB",
  "entityType": "PRIVATE_LIMITED",
  "websiteUrl": "https://duplicate-merchant.com",
  "primaryCurrency": "USD",
  "primaryContactEmail": "dup@testmerchant.com",
  "primaryContactName": "Dup User",
  "registeredAddress": {
    "streetLine1": "456 Dup Street",
    "city": "London",
    "postcode": "EC1A 1BB",
    "country": "GB"
  },
  "beneficialOwners": [
    {
      "fullName": "Dup User",
      "dateOfBirth": "1990-01-01",
      "nationality": "GB",
      "ownershipPct": 100.00,
      "isPoliticallyExposed": false
    }
  ],
  "requestedCorridors": ["GB->US"]
}

request.description: Second submission of the same registration number (REG-DUPLICATE-FIXED-001). This one should return 409 Conflict.
